name: exact_domain_match
description: Battery Management System spec vs BMS patent. Same domain, high overlap. All three claim elements should appear in the spec with enough token overlap for MATCH. Acts as sanity baseline: if the heuristic scores well anywhere it should be here.
target_spec:
Battery Management System for Lithium-Ion Packs

This system monitors and protects a lithium-ion battery pack. A voltage sensor measures cell voltage across each cell in the pack and reports measurements to the control unit. A temperature sensor monitors cell temperature to prevent thermal runaway. The control unit processes voltage and temperature readings and controls a protection circuit that disconnects the battery pack if voltage or temperature exceeds safe limits. A state-of-charge estimator computes remaining capacity from measured voltage and current.

patent.title: Battery management system with integrated cell voltage and temperature protection
claims: 1. A battery management system comprising a voltage sensor configured to measure cell voltage of a battery pack; a temperature sensor configured to monitor cell temperature; and a control unit configured to control a protection circuit based on the measured cell voltage and the monitored cell temperature.
2. The system of claim 1, wherein the control unit disconnects the battery pack when the cell voltage exceeds a threshold.

expected_verdicts.rationale: spec explicitly says 'A voltage sensor measures cell voltage' — direct vocabulary match
spec explicitly says 'A temperature sensor monitors cell temperature' — direct vocabulary match
spec says 'control unit processes voltage and temperature readings and controls a protection circuit' — control unit and protection circuit both present
